title: Crispy Caramelized Mediterranean Thai Eggplant Flatbread
description: A solo dinner Mediterranean dish featuring Thai eggplant makhua with a jammy and flaky texture, this flatbread combines the flavors of the Mediterranean with the wok-hei of Thai cuisine, making it a dash-friendly meal option.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
Slice the Thai eggplant into 1/2 inch thick rounds and brush with olive oil.
Season the eggplant with salt and black pepper, then roast in the oven for 20 minutes.
Meanwhile, heat a wok or large skillet over high heat and add a small amount of oil.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the roasted eggplant to the wok and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Brush the flatbread with olive oil and top with the eggplant mixture, crumbly feta cheese, and chopped parsley.
Drizzle with lemon juice and season with salt and black pepper.
Bake the flatbread in the oven for 10 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 2 minutes before slicing and serving.
